hypothesis: Replacing composition-weighted additive aggregation with a permutation-invariant attention-based interaction encoder over constituent SMI-TED molecular embeddings and mole fractions will capture salt–solvent interactions sufficiently to raise electrolyte solubility prediction accuracy by ≥1 percentage point and lower log ionic conductivity MAE by ≥10% relative to Morgan Fingerprints on the same datasets, including tail-end out-of-distribution splits.
